channel.title: Recent Articles in Phys. Rev. Lett.
channel.description: Recent articles in Physical Review Letters
channel.dc:creator: rss@aps.org
channel.dc:rights: Copyright © 2026 the American Physical Society. Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Fluxoid Solitons in Superconducting Tapered Tubes and Bottlenecks
item.description:
Author(s): Tim Kokkeler, Mateo Uldemolins, Francisco Lobo, F. Sebastian Bergeret, Elsa Prada, and Pablo San-Jose

A fluxoid soliton is a new type of topologically protected state that appears in thin-walled tubular superconductors.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086001] Published Thu Feb 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Tim Kokkeler, Mateo Uldemolins, Francisco Lobo, F. Sebastian Bergeret, Elsa Prada, and Pablo San-Jose

A fluxoid soliton is a new type of topologically protected state that appears in thin-walled tubular superconductors.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086001] Published Thu Feb 26, 2026
item.dc:title: Fluxoid Solitons in Superconducting Tapered Tubes and Bottlenecks
item.dc:creator: Tim Kokkeler, Mateo Uldemolins, Francisco Lobo, F. Sebastian Bergeret, Elsa Prada, and Pablo San-Jose
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Topological Chiral Superconductivity in the Triangular-Lattice Hofstadter-Hubbard Model
item.description:
Author(s): Feng Chen, Wen O. Wang, Jia-Xin Zhang, Leon Balents, and D. N. Sheng

Moiré materials provide exciting platforms for studying the interplay of strong electronic correlation and large magnetic flux effects. We study the lightly doped Hofstadter-Hubbard model on a triangular lattice through the large-scale density matrix renormalization group and determinantal quantum M…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086503] Published Thu Feb 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Feng Chen, Wen O. Wang, Jia-Xin Zhang, Leon Balents, and D. N. Sheng

Moiré materials provide exciting platforms for studying the interplay of strong electronic correlation and large magnetic flux effects. We study the lightly doped Hofstadter-Hubbard model on a triangular lattice through the large-scale density matrix renormalization group and determinantal quantum M…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086503] Published Thu Feb 26, 2026
item.dc:title: Topological Chiral Superconductivity in the Triangular-Lattice Hofstadter-Hubbard Model
item.dc:creator: Feng Chen, Wen O. Wang, Jia-Xin Zhang, Leon Balents, and D. N. Sheng
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Dynamics of Current-Induced Switching in the Quantum Anomalous Hall Effect
item.description:
Author(s): Alina Rupp, Daniel Rosenbach, Torsten Röper, Dominik Hoborka, Alexey A. Taskin, Yoichi Ando, and Erwann Bocquillon

Ferromagnetic topological insulators in the quantum anomalous Hall (QAH) regime host chiral, dissipationless edge states whose propagation direction is determined by the internal magnetization. Under suitable conditions, a strong electrical bias can induce magnetization reversal, and thus flip the p…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086602] Published Thu Feb 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Alina Rupp, Daniel Rosenbach, Torsten Röper, Dominik Hoborka, Alexey A. Taskin, Yoichi Ando, and Erwann Bocquillon

Ferromagnetic topological insulators in the quantum anomalous Hall (QAH) regime host chiral, dissipationless edge states whose propagation direction is determined by the internal magnetization. Under suitable conditions, a strong electrical bias can induce magnetization reversal, and thus flip the p…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086602] Published Thu Feb 26, 2026
item.dc:title: Dynamics of Current-Induced Switching in the Quantum Anomalous Hall Effect
item.dc:creator: Alina Rupp, Daniel Rosenbach, Torsten Röper, Dominik Hoborka, Alexey A. Taskin, Yoichi Ando, and Erwann Bocquillon
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Bilayer Graphene Quantum Dots as a Quantum Simulator of Haldane Topological Quantum Matter
item.description:
Author(s): Daniel Miravet, Hassan Allami, Marek Korkusiński, and Paweł Hawrylak

We demonstrate here that a chain of bilayer graphene quantum dots (BLGQDs) can realize topological quantum matter by effectively simulating a spin-1 chain that hosts the Haldane phase within a specific range of parameters. We describe a chain of BLGQDs with two electrons per dot using an atomistic t…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086604] Published Thu Feb 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Daniel Miravet, Hassan Allami, Marek Korkusiński, and Paweł Hawrylak

We demonstrate here that a chain of bilayer graphene quantum dots (BLGQDs) can realize topological quantum matter by effectively simulating a spin-1 chain that hosts the Haldane phase within a specific range of parameters. We describe a chain of BLGQDs with two electrons per dot using an atomistic t…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086604] Published Thu Feb 26, 2026
item.dc:title: Bilayer Graphene Quantum Dots as a Quantum Simulator of Haldane Topological Quantum Matter
item.dc:creator: Daniel Miravet, Hassan Allami, Marek Korkusiński, and Paweł Hawrylak
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Suppression of Thermal Conductivity via Singlet-Dominated Scattering in ${\mathrm{TmFeO}}_{3}$
item.description:
Author(s): M. L. McLanahan, D. Lederman, and A. P. Ramirez

We measured the thermal conductivity of the rare-earth orthoferrites, $R{\mathrm{FeO}}_{3}$, where $R=\mathrm{Eu}$, Gd, Tb, Dy, Ho, Er, Tm, and Yb from 3 to 300 K and see an anomalous strong suppression for ${\mathrm{TmFeO}}_{3}$ over most of the temperature range. Using a Debye thermal transport mo…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086705] Published Thu Feb 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): M. L. McLanahan, D. Lederman, and A. P. Ramirez

We measured the thermal conductivity of the rare-earth orthoferrites, RFeO3, where R=Eu, Gd, Tb, Dy, Ho, Er, Tm, and Yb from 3 to 300 K and see an anomalous strong suppression for TmFeO3 over most of the temperature range. Using a Debye thermal transport model, we demonstrate that this suppression i…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086705] Published Thu Feb 26, 2026
item.dc:title: Suppression of Thermal Conductivity via Singlet-Dominated Scattering in ${\mathrm{TmFeO}}_{3}$
item.dc:creator: M. L. McLanahan, D. Lederman, and A. P. Ramirez
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Manipulating Charge Distribution in Moiré Superlattices by Light
item.description:
Author(s): Ruiping Guo, Haowei Chen, Wenhui Duan, Yong Xu, and Chong Wang

Uniform optical illumination is predicted to induce static but spatially inhomogeneous charge redistribution within a supercell in moiré superlattices.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086903] Published Thu Feb 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ruiping Guo, Haowei Chen, Wenhui Duan, Yong Xu, and Chong Wang

Uniform optical illumination is predicted to induce static but spatially inhomogeneous charge redistribution within a supercell in moiré superlattices.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086903] Published Thu Feb 26, 2026
item.dc:title: Manipulating Charge Distribution in Moiré Superlattices by Light
item.dc:creator: Ruiping Guo, Haowei Chen, Wenhui Duan, Yong Xu, and Chong Wang
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Minimal Theory of Strange Carriers
item.description:
Author(s): Simone Fratini

I explore a theory of transport and optical properties of strange metallic carriers in strongly correlated systems that follows from assuming that the diffusion constant has reached its quantum limit $D=ℏ/m$, and that such quantum carriers behave as distinguishable particles as they would in an elec…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086301] Published Wed Feb 25, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Simone Fratini

I explore a theory of transport and optical properties of strange metallic carriers in strongly correlated systems that follows from assuming that the diffusion constant has reached its quantum limit D=ℏ/m, and that such quantum carriers behave as distinguishable particles as they would in an electr…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086301] Published Wed Feb 25, 2026
item.dc:title: Minimal Theory of Strange Carriers
item.dc:creator: Simone Fratini
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Van Hove Singularities, Superconductivity, and the Josephson Diode Effect in ${\mathrm{NiTe}}_{2}$ and ${\mathrm{PdTe}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Emily C. McFarlane, Antonio Sanna, Matthew J. Gilbert, Jonas A. Krieger, Mihir Date, Gabriele Domaine, Banabir Pal, Anirban Chakraborty, Pranava K. Sivakumar, Procopios C. Constantinou, Anna Hartl, Enrico G. Della Valle, Camilla Pellegrini, Vladimir N. Strocov, Stuart S. P. Parkin, and Niels B. M. Schröter

Superconductivity in the transition-metal dichalcogenide ${\mathrm{PdTe}}_{2}$ has been attributed to the proximity of a three-dimensional Van Hove singularity to the Fermi level. In isostructural ${\mathrm{NiTe}}_{2}$, recently used as the weak link in a Josephson diode, a similar Van Hove singular…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086401] Published Wed Feb 25, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Emily C. McFarlane, Antonio Sanna, Matthew J. Gilbert, Jonas A. Krieger, Mihir Date, Gabriele Domaine, Banabir Pal, Anirban Chakraborty, Pranava K. Sivakumar, Procopios C. Constantinou, Anna Hartl, Enrico G. Della Valle, Camilla Pellegrini, Vladimir N. Strocov, Stuart S. P. Parkin, and Niels B. M. Schröter

Superconductivity in the transition-metal dichalcogenide PdTe2 has been attributed to the proximity of a three-dimensional Van Hove singularity to the Fermi level. In isostructural NiTe2, recently used as the weak link in a Josephson diode, a similar Van Hove singularity has been predicted to occur,…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086401] Published Wed Feb 25, 2026
item.dc:title: Van Hove Singularities, Superconductivity, and the Josephson Diode Effect in ${\mathrm{NiTe}}_{2}$ and ${\mathrm{PdTe}}_{2}$
item.dc:creator: Emily C. McFarlane, Antonio Sanna, Matthew J. Gilbert, Jonas A. Krieger, Mihir Date, Gabriele Domaine, Banabir Pal, Anirban Chakraborty, Pranava K. Sivakumar, Procopios C. Constantinou, Anna Hartl, Enrico G. Della Valle, Camilla Pellegrini, Vladimir N. Strocov, Stuart S. P. Parkin, and Niels B. M. Schröter
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Hydrogen Vacancy Induced Superconductivity Collapse in A15 Lanthanum Hydride
item.description:
Author(s): Israel Osmond, Lewis J. Conway, Mikhail A. Kuzovnikov, Callum Stevens, Tomas Marqueño, Hannah A. Shuttleworth, Andrew Huxley, Chris J. Pickard, Graeme J. Ackland, Ross T. Howie, and Miriam Peña-Alvarez

The first direct demonstration that hydrogen content alone drives the superconductor-to-insulator transition in A15-type lanthanum hydride provides a benchmark for tunable high-Tc superconductivity at reduced pressures.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086102] Published Tue Feb 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Israel Osmond, Lewis J. Conway, Mikhail A. Kuzovnikov, Callum Stevens, Tomas Marqueño, Hannah A. Shuttleworth, Andrew Huxley, Chris J. Pickard, Graeme J. Ackland, Ross T. Howie, and Miriam Peña-Alvarez

The first direct demonstration that hydrogen content alone drives the superconductor-to-insulator transition in A15-type lanthanum hydride provides a benchmark for tunable high-Tc superconductivity at reduced pressures.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086102] Published Tue Feb 24, 2026
item.dc:title: Hydrogen Vacancy Induced Superconductivity Collapse in A15 Lanthanum Hydride
item.dc:creator: Israel Osmond, Lewis J. Conway, Mikhail A. Kuzovnikov, Callum Stevens, Tomas Marqueño, Hannah A. Shuttleworth, Andrew Huxley, Chris J. Pickard, Graeme J. Ackland, Ross T. Howie, and Miriam Peña-Alvarez
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Modulation of Superconductivity across a Lifshitz Transition in Alternating-Angle Twisted Quadrilayer Graphene
item.description:
Author(s): Isabelle Y. Phinney, Andrew Zimmerman, Zeyu Hao, Patrick J. Ledwith, Takashi Taniguchi, Kenji Watanabe, Ashvin Vishwanath, and Philip Kim

Magnetotransport and electric field both tune and probe the band structure of twisted quadrilayer graphene while simultaneously measuring the superconducting properties of the system.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086501] Published Tue Feb 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Isabelle Y. Phinney, Andrew Zimmerman, Zeyu Hao, Patrick J. Ledwith, Takashi Taniguchi, Kenji Watanabe, Ashvin Vishwanath, and Philip Kim

Magnetotransport and electric field both tune and probe the band structure of twisted quadrilayer graphene while simultaneously measuring the superconducting properties of the system.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086501] Published Tue Feb 24, 2026
item.dc:title: Modulation of Superconductivity across a Lifshitz Transition in Alternating-Angle Twisted Quadrilayer Graphene
item.dc:creator: Isabelle Y. Phinney, Andrew Zimmerman, Zeyu Hao, Patrick J. Ledwith, Takashi Taniguchi, Kenji Watanabe, Ashvin Vishwanath, and Philip Kim
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Nonequilibrium Dynamics of Dirac Quantum Criticality in Imaginary Time
item.description:
Author(s): Yin-Kai Yu, Zhi Zeng, Yu-Rong Shu, Zi-Xiang Li, and Shuai Yin

Quantum criticality within Dirac fermions harbors a plethora of exotic phenomena, attracting sustained attention in the past decades. Here, we explore the imaginary-time relaxation dynamics in a typical Dirac quantum criticality belonging to chiral Heisenberg universality class. Performing large-sca…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086502] Published Tue Feb 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yin-Kai Yu, Zhi Zeng, Yu-Rong Shu, Zi-Xiang Li, and Shuai Yin

Quantum criticality within Dirac fermions harbors a plethora of exotic phenomena, attracting sustained attention in the past decades. Here, we explore the imaginary-time relaxation dynamics in a typical Dirac quantum criticality belonging to chiral Heisenberg universality class. Performing large-sca…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086502] Published Tue Feb 24, 2026
item.dc:title: Nonequilibrium Dynamics of Dirac Quantum Criticality in Imaginary Time
item.dc:creator: Yin-Kai Yu, Zhi Zeng, Yu-Rong Shu, Zi-Xiang Li, and Shuai Yin
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Incommensurate Magnetism Drives Singular Angular Magnetoresistance in the Magnetic Weyl Semimetal CeAlGe
item.description:
Author(s): X. Yao, P. Chen, R. Verma, X. Zhao, H.-Y. Yang, L. DeBeer-Schmitt, A. A. Aczel, C.-M. Wu, D. Alba Venero, T. Ohhara, K. Munakata, M. Takahashi, Y. Noda, A. Bansil, B. Singh, P. Nikolić, F. Tafti, and J. Gaudet

We demonstrate that a multi-$\mathbf{k}$ incommensurate magnetic state in the Weyl semimetal CeAlGe gives rise to a singular angular magnetoresistance (SAMR), which is an electrical transport signature capable of detecting magnetic field direction with exceptional precision. In contrast, its sister …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086702] Published Tue Feb 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): X. Yao, P. Chen, R. Verma, X. Zhao, H.-Y. Yang, L. DeBeer-Schmitt, A. A. Aczel, C.-M. Wu, D. Alba Venero, T. Ohhara, K. Munakata, M. Takahashi, Y. Noda, A. Bansil, B. Singh, P. Nikolić, F. Tafti, and J. Gaudet

We demonstrate that a multi-k incommensurate magnetic state in the Weyl semimetal CeAlGe gives rise to a singular angular magnetoresistance (SAMR), which is an electrical transport signature capable of detecting magnetic field direction with exceptional precision. In contrast, its sister compound Ce…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086702] Published Tue Feb 24, 2026
item.dc:title: Incommensurate Magnetism Drives Singular Angular Magnetoresistance in the Magnetic Weyl Semimetal CeAlGe
item.dc:creator: X. Yao, P. Chen, R. Verma, X. Zhao, H.-Y. Yang, L. DeBeer-Schmitt, A. A. Aczel, C.-M. Wu, D. Alba Venero, T. Ohhara, K. Munakata, M. Takahashi, Y. Noda, A. Bansil, B. Singh, P. Nikolić, F. Tafti, and J. Gaudet
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Interaction-Driven Altermagnetic Magnon Chiral Splitting
item.description:
Author(s): Zhejunyu Jin, Zhaozhuo Zeng, Jie Liu, Tianci Gong, Ying Su, Kai Chang, and Peng Yan

Nonrelativistic magnon chiral splitting in altermagnets has garnered significant recent attention. In this Letter, we demonstrate that nonlinear three-wave mixing—where magnons split or coalesce—extends this phenomenon into unprecedented relativistic regimes. Employing a bilayer antiferromagnet with…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086703] Published Tue Feb 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Zhejunyu Jin, Zhaozhuo Zeng, Jie Liu, Tianci Gong, Ying Su, Kai Chang, and Peng Yan

Nonrelativistic magnon chiral splitting in altermagnets has garnered significant recent attention. In this Letter, we demonstrate that nonlinear three-wave mixing—where magnons split or coalesce—extends this phenomenon into unprecedented relativistic regimes. Employing a bilayer antiferromagnet with…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086703] Published Tue Feb 24, 2026
item.dc:title: Interaction-Driven Altermagnetic Magnon Chiral Splitting
item.dc:creator: Zhejunyu Jin, Zhaozhuo Zeng, Jie Liu, Tianci Gong, Ying Su, Kai Chang, and Peng Yan
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: High-Order Anisotropic Magnetoresistance in a Cubic Ferromagnet
item.description:
Author(s): Haoran Chen, Yue Chen, Yizi Feng, Ruda Guo, Yuanfei Fan, Hongyue Xu, Tong Wu, Zhongxun Guo, Di Yue, Xiaofeng Jin, Yi Liu, Zhe Yuan, and Yizheng Wu

Careful Fourier analysis resolves harmonics of a higher order than 4, up to the 18th order, in cubic iron films.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086704] Published Tue Feb 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Haoran Chen, Yue Chen, Yizi Feng, Ruda Guo, Yuanfei Fan, Hongyue Xu, Tong Wu, Zhongxun Guo, Di Yue, Xiaofeng Jin, Yi Liu, Zhe Yuan, and Yizheng Wu

Careful Fourier analysis resolves harmonics of a higher order than 4, up to the 18th order, in cubic iron films.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086704] Published Tue Feb 24, 2026
item.dc:title: High-Order Anisotropic Magnetoresistance in a Cubic Ferromagnet
item.dc:creator: Haoran Chen, Yue Chen, Yizi Feng, Ruda Guo, Yuanfei Fan, Hongyue Xu, Tong Wu, Zhongxun Guo, Di Yue, Xiaofeng Jin, Yi Liu, Zhe Yuan, and Yizheng Wu
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Anyonic Membranes and Pontryagin Statistics
item.description:
Author(s): Yitao Feng (冯逸韬), Hanyu Xue (薛寒玉), Yuyang Li (李雨阳), Meng Cheng (程蒙), Ryohei Kobayashi (小林良平), Po-Shen Hsin (辛柏伸), and Yu-An Chen (陳昱安)

Anyons, unique to two spatial dimensions, underlie extraordinary phenomena such as the fractional quantum Hall effect, but their generalization to higher dimensions has remained elusive. The topology of Eilenberg-MacLane spaces constrains the loop statistics to be only bosonic or fermionic in any di…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086601] Published Mon Feb 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yitao Feng (冯逸韬), Hanyu Xue (薛寒玉), Yuyang Li (李雨阳), Meng Cheng (程蒙), Ryohei Kobayashi (小林良平), Po-Shen Hsin (辛柏伸), and Yu-An Chen (陳昱安)

Anyons, unique to two spatial dimensions, underlie extraordinary phenomena such as the fractional quantum Hall effect, but their generalization to higher dimensions has remained elusive. The topology of Eilenberg-MacLane spaces constrains the loop statistics to be only bosonic or fermionic in any di…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086601] Published Mon Feb 23, 2026
item.dc:title: Anyonic Membranes and Pontryagin Statistics
item.dc:creator: Yitao Feng (冯逸韬), Hanyu Xue (薛寒玉), Yuyang Li (李雨阳), Meng Cheng (程蒙), Ryohei Kobayashi (小林良平), Po-Shen Hsin (辛柏伸), and Yu-An Chen (陳昱安)
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Topological Devil’s Staircase in a Constrained Kagome Ising Antiferromagnet
item.description:
Author(s): Afonso Rufino, Samuel Nyckees, Jeanne Colbois, and Frédéric Mila

We show that the constrained Ising model on the kagome lattice with infinite first and third neighbor couplings undergoes an infinite series of thermal first-order transitions at which, as in the Kasteleyn transition, linear defects of infinite length condense. However, their density undergoes abrup…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086701] Published Mon Feb 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Afonso Rufino, Samuel Nyckees, Jeanne Colbois, and Frédéric Mila

We show that the constrained Ising model on the kagome lattice with infinite first and third neighbor couplings undergoes an infinite series of thermal first-order transitions at which, as in the Kasteleyn transition, linear defects of infinite length condense. However, their density undergoes abrup…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086701] Published Mon Feb 23, 2026
item.dc:title: Topological Devil’s Staircase in a Constrained Kagome Ising Antiferromagnet
item.dc:creator: Afonso Rufino, Samuel Nyckees, Jeanne Colbois, and Frédéric Mila
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: (Ir)Relevance of Disorder for Superconductivity in Cuprates
item.description:
Author(s): R. Caruso, X. He, A. T. Bollinger, and I. Božović

In cuprates that exhibit high-temperature superconductivity (HTS), as the doping level $p$ is increased beyond the optimal value, the superfluid density ${n}_{s0}$ decreases and eventually vanishes, closely tracking the critical temperature ${T}_{c}$. This has been interpreted using a dirty-$d$-wave…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 076003] Published Fri Feb 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): R. Caruso, X. He, A. T. Bollinger, and I. Božović

In cuprates that exhibit high-temperature superconductivity (HTS), as the doping level p is increased beyond the optimal value, the superfluid density ns0 decreases and eventually vanishes, closely tracking the critical temperature Tc. This has been interpreted using a dirty-d-wave extension of the …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 076003] Published Fri Feb 20, 2026
item.dc:title: (Ir)Relevance of Disorder for Superconductivity in Cuprates
item.dc:creator: R. Caruso, X. He, A. T. Bollinger, and I. Božović
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Nanoscale Observation and Control of Quasiparticle Induced Magnetic Noise in a Superconducting Resonator
item.description:
Author(s): Senlei Li, Shane P. Kelly, Jingcheng Zhou, Hanyi Lu, Yaroslav Tserkovnyak, Hailong Wang, and Chunhui Rita Du

Superconducting circuits are arguably taking a leading role in driving the ongoing quantum technological revolution. A detailed knowledge of the microscopic fluctuating electromagnetic properties plays an important role in advancing the circuitry design, testing, and material integration of cutting-…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 076004] Published Fri Feb 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Senlei Li, Shane P. Kelly, Jingcheng Zhou, Hanyi Lu, Yaroslav Tserkovnyak, Hailong Wang, and Chunhui Rita Du

Superconducting circuits are arguably taking a leading role in driving the ongoing quantum technological revolution. A detailed knowledge of the microscopic fluctuating electromagnetic properties plays an important role in advancing the circuitry design, testing, and material integration of cutting-…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 076004] Published Fri Feb 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Nanoscale Observation and Control of Quasiparticle Induced Magnetic Noise in a Superconducting Resonator
item.dc:creator: Senlei Li, Shane P. Kelly, Jingcheng Zhou, Hanyi Lu, Yaroslav Tserkovnyak, Hailong Wang, and Chunhui Rita Du
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Conformal Operator Flows of the Deconfined Quantum Criticality from $\mathrm{SO}(5)$ to $\mathrm{O}(4)$
item.description:
Author(s): Shuai Yang, Liang-dong Hu, Chao Han, W. Zhu, and Yan Chen

The deconfined quantum critical point (DQCP), which separates two distinct symmetry-broken phases, was conjectured to be an example of $(2+1)\mathrm{D}$ criticality beyond the standard Landau-Ginzburg-Wilson paradigm. However, this hypothesis has been met with challenges and remains elusive. Here, w…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 076505] Published Fri Feb 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Shuai Yang, Liang-dong Hu, Chao Han, W. Zhu, and Yan Chen

The deconfined quantum critical point (DQCP), which separates two distinct symmetry-broken phases, was conjectured to be an example of (2+1)D criticality beyond the standard Landau-Ginzburg-Wilson paradigm. However, this hypothesis has been met with challenges and remains elusive. Here, we perform a…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 076505] Published Fri Feb 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Conformal Operator Flows of the Deconfined Quantum Criticality from $\mathrm{SO}(5)$ to $\mathrm{O}(4)$
item.dc:creator: Shuai Yang, Liang-dong Hu, Chao Han, W. Zhu, and Yan Chen
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Critical Majorana Fermion at a Topological Quantum Hall Bilayer Transition
item.description:
Author(s): Cristian Voinea, Wei Zhu, Nicolas Regnault, and Zlatko Papić

Quantum Hall bilayers are a uniquely tunable platform that can realize continuous transitions between distinct topological phases of matter. One prominent example is the transition between the Halperin state and the Moore-Read Pfaffian, long predicted to host a critical theory of Majorana fermions b…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 076601] Published Fri Feb 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Cristian Voinea, Wei Zhu, Nicolas Regnault, and Zlatko Papić

Quantum Hall bilayers are a uniquely tunable platform that can realize continuous transitions between distinct topological phases of matter. One prominent example is the transition between the Halperin state and the Moore-Read Pfaffian, long predicted to host a critical theory of Majorana fermions b…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 076601] Published Fri Feb 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Critical Majorana Fermion at a Topological Quantum Hall Bilayer Transition
item.dc:creator: Cristian Voinea, Wei Zhu, Nicolas Regnault, and Zlatko Papić
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Theory of Stimulated and Spontaneous Axion Scattering
item.description:
Author(s): M. Smith, Kartiek Agarwal, and Ivar Martin

We present a theory for nonlinear, resonant excitation of dynamical axions by counterpropagating electromagnetic waves in materials that break both $\mathcal{P}$ and $\mathcal{T}$ symmetries. We show that dynamical axions can mediate an exponential growth in the amplitude of the lower frequency (Sto…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 076903] Published Fri Feb 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): M. Smith, Kartiek Agarwal, and Ivar Martin

We present a theory for nonlinear, resonant excitation of dynamical axions by counterpropagating electromagnetic waves in materials that break both P and T symmetries. We show that dynamical axions can mediate an exponential growth in the amplitude of the lower frequency (Stokes) beam. We also discu…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 076903] Published Fri Feb 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Theory of Stimulated and Spontaneous Axion Scattering
item.dc:creator: M. Smith, Kartiek Agarwal, and Ivar Martin
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Direct Loading of BaF Molecules with a Conveyor-Belt Magneto-optical Trap
item.description:
Author(s): Zixuan Zeng, Shoukang Yang, Shuhua Deng, and Bo Yan

We report the realization of a blue-detuned magneto-optical trap (BDM) of BaF molecules. The ($1+1$) type BDM and ($1+2$) type conveyor-belt MOT (CB-MOT) are explored. While the ($1+1$) BDM provides only a weak trapping force, the CB-MOT significantly compresses the molecular cloud, achieving a Gaus…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 073402] Published Thu Feb 19, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Zixuan Zeng, Shoukang Yang, Shuhua Deng, and Bo Yan

We report the realization of a blue-detuned magneto-optical trap (BDM) of BaF molecules. The (1+1) type BDM and (1+2) type conveyor-belt MOT (CB-MOT) are explored. While the (1+1) BDM provides only a weak trapping force, the CB-MOT significantly compresses the molecular cloud, achieving a Gaussian r…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 073402] Published Thu Feb 19, 2026
item.dc:title: Direct Loading of BaF Molecules with a Conveyor-Belt Magneto-optical Trap
item.dc:creator: Zixuan Zeng, Shoukang Yang, Shuhua Deng, and Bo Yan
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Atomic, Molecular, and Optical Physics

item.title: Questioning the Cuprate Paradigm: Absence of Superfluid Density Loss in Several Overdoped Cuprates I
item.description:
Author(s): J. L. Tallon, J. G. Storey, J. W. Loram, Jianlin Luo, C. Bernhard, I. Kokanović, and J. R. Cooper

It is long established that overdoped cuprate superconductors experience a loss of superfluid density (SFD) with increasing doping, $p$, along with the decline in ${T}_{c}$. Such behavior is unconventional and suggests a depletion of the condensate by increasing pairbreaking or the growth of a secon…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 076002] Published Thu Feb 19, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): J. L. Tallon, J. G. Storey, J. W. Loram, Jianlin Luo, C. Bernhard, I. Kokanović, and J. R. Cooper

It is long established that overdoped cuprate superconductors experience a loss of superfluid density (SFD) with increasing doping, p, along with the decline in Tc. Such behavior is unconventional and suggests a depletion of the condensate by increasing pairbreaking or the growth of a second nonpair…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 076002] Published Thu Feb 19, 2026
item.dc:title: Questioning the Cuprate Paradigm: Absence of Superfluid Density Loss in Several Overdoped Cuprates I
item.dc:creator: J. L. Tallon, J. G. Storey, J. W. Loram, Jianlin Luo, C. Bernhard, I. Kokanović, and J. R. Cooper
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Quintuplet Condensation in the Skyrmionic Insulator ${\mathrm{Cu}}_{2}{\mathrm{OSeO}}_{3}$ at Ultrahigh Magnetic Fields
item.description:
Author(s): T. Nomura, I. Rousochatzakis, O. Janson, M. Gen, X.-G. Zhou, Y. Ishii, S. Seki, Y. Kohama, and Y. H. Matsuda

Faraday rotation at ultrahigh magnetic fields uncovers magnon Bose–Einstein condensation in a chiral helimagnet


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 076703] Published Thu Feb 19, 2026

Faraday rotation at ultrahigh magnetic fields uncovers magnon Bose–Einstein condensation in a chiral helimagnet


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 076703] Published Thu Feb 19, 2026

item.title: Instantaneous Optical Selection Rule for Independent Control of Valley Currents
item.description:
Author(s): Wanzhu He, Xiaosong Zhu, Liang Li, Di Wu, Xiaotong Zhu, Pengfei Lan, and Peixiang Lu

We reveal an instantaneous optical valley selection rule that illuminates the coupling between the instantaneous optical chirality of the driving laser field and the chirality of valley systems. Building on this principle, we propose and demonstrate that a single chirality-separated optical field, i…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 076902] Published Thu Feb 19, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Wanzhu He, Xiaosong Zhu, Liang Li, Di Wu, Xiaotong Zhu, Pengfei Lan, and Peixiang Lu

We reveal an instantaneous optical valley selection rule that illuminates the coupling between the instantaneous optical chirality of the driving laser field and the chirality of valley systems. Building on this principle, we propose and demonstrate that a single chirality-separated optical field, i…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 076902] Published Thu Feb 19, 2026
item.dc:title: Instantaneous Optical Selection Rule for Independent Control of Valley Currents
